skill.name: n8n-node-configuration
skill.description: Guides operation-aware n8n node configuration, property dependency discovery, required field selection, and iterative validation workflows.
skill.summary: Configure n8n nodes by operation, dependency rules, and validation feedback.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution and weak cryptography patterns. Review found Markdown inline code, code fences, and n8n configuration examples instead of executable logic. The hardcoded URLs are a source link and api.example.com examples, with no fetch, shell, environment, or exfiltration behavior found.

low_findings.title: Static Findings Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The external command detections are caused by Markdown inline code and JavaScript examples such as get_node and validate_node. The weak cryptography detections do not correspond to cryptographic API usage in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown prose or fenced JavaScript examples. No shell execution, subprocess API, or cryptographic implementation is present in the reviewed contexts.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Non-Executable Examples
low_findings.description: The network detections are the upstream source URL and placeholder api.example.com endpoints inside configuration examples. They are not used by code that performs network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear in frontmatter or sample node configuration values. The file contains no fetch, HTTP client call, shell command, or other execution path using these URLs.

content.user_title: Configure n8n Nodes with Operation Context
content.value_statement: n8n nodes often hide required fields behind operation and dependency rules. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through discovery, validation, and minimal configuration.
actual_capabilities: Explains how resource and operation choices change required n8n fields.
Recommends when to use standard, full, or search property node discovery.
Shows common dependency patterns for HTTP, Slack, database, IF, Switch, and Merge nodes.
Guides iterative validation with validate_node before workflow deployment.
Identifies when optional fields should be added after minimal configuration works.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts or direct n8n API access.
Does not replace validation against the target n8n instance.
May not cover custom community nodes with undocumented properties.
Assumes the user has access to the relevant n8n discovery and validation tools.

use_cases.title: Build reliable workflow nodes
use_cases.description: Configure n8n nodes with the correct required fields for each selected resource and operation.

use_cases.title: Debug validation failures
use_cases.description: Use validation errors and property search to find missing conditional fields.

use_cases.title: Standardize integration patterns
use_cases.description: Document repeatable configuration rules for HTTP, database, messaging, and conditional logic nodes.

prompt_templates.title: Configure a basic node
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for one n8n node.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me configure an n8n node for this goal. Identify the node type, resource, operation, and minimal required fields first.

prompt_templates.title: Resolve missing fields
prompt_templates.scenario: Validation fails because required fields are unclear.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this n8n validation error and explain which operation or dependency probably controls the missing field.

prompt_templates.title: Compare detail levels
prompt_templates.scenario: Choose the right discovery method before requesting schema data.
prompt_templates.prompt: Decide whether I should use standard detail, search_properties, or full detail for this n8n configuration task.

prompt_templates.title: Design a reusable configuration workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow design across several node types.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an operation-aware configuration plan for these n8n nodes. Include discovery order, validation checkpoints, and dependency risks.

output_examples.input: Configure an HTTP Request node that sends JSON to an API.
output_examples.output: The response starts with the HTTP method and URL, then adds body settings only after the POST operation requires them.

output_examples.input: A Slack update operation fails after copying a post message configuration.
output_examples.output: The response explains that update operations require a message identifier, while post operations require a channel.

output_examples.input: A field is missing from standard node discovery.
output_examples.output: The response recommends property search first, then full detail only if the dependency rules are still unclear.

best_practices: Start with standard node discovery before requesting full schemas.
Validate after each meaningful configuration change.
Recheck required fields whenever the resource or operation changes.
anti_patterns: Do not copy a node configuration across operations without checking requirements.
Do not request full schema detail before standard discovery fails to answer the question.
Do not deploy a workflow before validating operation-specific required fields.

faq.question: Does this skill run n8n workflows?
faq.answer: No. It provides configuration guidance and validation workflow advice only.

faq.question: Which n8n nodes does it discuss?
faq.answer: It covers common patterns for HTTP Request, Slack, database, IF, Switch, Merge, and similar nodes.

faq.question: When should I use full node detail?
faq.answer: Use full detail only when standard discovery and property search do not explain the required fields.

faq.question: Can it help with validation errors?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains how validation errors often reveal missing operation-specific or dependency-controlled fields.

faq.question: Does it store credentials?
faq.answer: No. The skill text does not include credential storage or credential access logic.

faq.question: Is it useful for custom nodes?
faq.answer: It can help with general discovery strategy, but custom nodes may need additional schema inspection.
